stories.headline: US Vice-President JD Vance says talks failed to reach agreement with Iran
stories.summary: US Vice-President JD Vance announced that talks between the US and Iran have failed to reach an agreement. The 21-hour peace talks, held in Islamabad, aimed to end the war between the two countries, but Iran refused to accept American terms. A key condition for the deal was Iran's commitment not to develop a nuclear weapon. Vance stated that the US needs to see an "affirmative commitment" from Iran that it will not pursue a nuclear weapon. The failure to reach a deal casts uncertainty over the fragile two-week cease-fire between the US and Iran. Vance had met with Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif ahead of the talks, which were seen as a historic opportunity for peace. The lack of an agreement raises concerns about the future of the cease-fire and the region's stability.

stories.headline: Vance says no peace deal reached with Iran to end war
stories.summary: US Vice-President JD Vance announced that 21 hours of negotiations with Iranian delegates in Pakistan have failed to produce a peace deal. The talks aimed to end the war between the US and Iran, but Tehran's demands, including the unblocking of Iranian assets and the end of sanctions, were not met. A ceasefire and the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz are now in doubt. The US and Israel stated that the Lebanon campaign is not part of the Iran-US ceasefire, while Iran insists it is. Despite the failure, Iran's top negotiator left the door open for future diplomacy. The breakdown in talks raises concerns about the potential escalation of the conflict. The US and Iran will likely continue to negotiate, but a resolution remains uncertain. The situation is being closely monitored for further developments.

stories.headline: Islamabad talks: U.S. and Iran fail to reach agreement after 21 hours, Vance departs Pakistan
stories.summary: US Vice President JD Vance and Iranian officials held 21-hour talks in Islamabad, Pakistan, but failed to reach a peace agreement to end the West Asia conflict. A key sticking point was Iran's refusal to abandon its nuclear program, which Vance cited as a major obstacle. Iranian delegates stated that Washington needs to do more to win their trust in order to resolve the US-Iran conflict. The Iranian foreign minister described Washington's demands as "excessive". The failure to reach an agreement raises doubts about the effectiveness of the talks and the potential for a ceasefire. Vance has departed Pakistan, leaving the status of the conflict uncertain. The talks were seen as a historic opportunity to end the war, but the lack of agreement has cast doubt on the prospects for a peaceful resolution.

stories.headline: Macron urges Iran to leverage Islamabad talks for peace
stories.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron has urged Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian to leverage the Islamabad talks for peace. The appeal came during a telephone conversation between the two leaders. High-stakes negotiations between the US and Iran took place in Islamabad, with the talks lasting several hours and confirmed by the White House to be face-to-face. The talks are part of Pakistan's efforts to broker a peace deal. The war between the US and Iran, which began on February 28, has resulted in nearly 3,000 Iranian deaths and at least 13 US servicemen killed, with dozens wounded. Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif met with Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, as delegations from the US and Iran held peace talks in Islamabad. Macron and Turkish President Erdogan are pushing for resolutions in Iran and Ukraine.

stories.headline: Microsoft pushes Windows 11 25H2 update as 24H2 support nears end
stories.summary: Microsoft has released the Windows 11 25H2 update, the next feature update in its annual release cycle. The company has begun rolling out the update, which is described as an "enablement package" with no significant under-the-hood changes or visual overhaul. The 25H2 update marks a shift in the company's OS lifecycle management, with an automated rollout to upgrade unmanaged Windows 11 Home and Pro devices from version 24H2 to 25H2. Microsoft has also released ISO install media for Windows 11 version 25H2 and issued the November 2025 Patch Tuesday update, which is the last patch update for Windows 11 23H2, as it is no longer supported. The 24H2 version is nearing its end of support, prompting the upgrade to 25H2, which has different support cycles for Enterprise, Education, and Pro editions.

stories.headline: Liquid AI Releases LFM2.5-VL-450M: a 450M-Parameter Vision-Language Model with Bounding Box Prediction, Multilingual Support, and Sub-250ms Edge Inference
stories.summary: Liquid AI has released LFM2.5-VL-450M, a 450M-parameter vision-language model with improved performance and new features. The model was pre-trained on 28T tokens and fine-tuned using preference optimization and reinforcement learning, resulting in consistent benchmark gains. It supports bounding box prediction, multilingual understanding, and text function calling, with improved performance on vision and language tasks. The model achieves sub-250ms edge inference, processing a 512×512 image in 242ms on NVIDIA Jetson Orin. Multilingual visual understanding has improved significantly, with MMMB scores rising to 68.09 across eight languages. The model is viable for global deployments without separate localization models, making it suitable for edge AI applications. LFM2.5-VL-450M outperforms its predecessor, LFM2-VL-450M, and other similar models in various benchmarks.

stories.headline: Your OpenClaw Mac Mini can now run larger local AI models, thanks to this officially approved eGPU driver
stories.summary: OpenClaw's Mac Mini can now run larger local AI models thanks to an officially approved eGPU driver. The driver enables the Mac Mini to tap into the power of a GPU, allowing for better and larger AI models to run on the device. Instructions for installation are available on the GitHub page. This development is significant for OpenClaw, which has gained popularity for enabling users to run personal AI agents on small, quiet, and low-power hardware. The Mac Mini M4 has become a preferred choice for self-hosted AI agents, with OpenClaw emerging as a leading solution. The eGPU driver approval allows users to unlock the full potential of their Mac Mini, enabling more complex AI models to run locally, enhancing the overall performance and capabilities of the device.

stories.headline: 100D Super Sabre for DCS World Nearing Release
stories.summary: The DCS: F-100D Super Sabre module by Grinnelli Designs is nearing release for DCS World. The F-100D was the first USAF fighter to achieve sustained supersonic, level flight, marking a significant milestone in the jet age. It played a crucial role in Vietnam, undertaking both fighter and ground-attack missions. With major development milestones complete, the module is in its final stages. Grinnelli Designs has released updates on the module's art, flight model, and sound development. The F-100D Super Sabre module is expected to bring a classic jet experience to DCS World, allowing players to strap into the cockpit and engage in realistic combat scenarios. Eagle Dynamics has confirmed the module's nearing release, generating excitement among fans and players of the DCS World series. The release date has not been officially announced, but progress is reportedly very good.

stories.headline: Rajasthan Royals breach the ultimate IPL protocol, manager in the firing line after video catches him red-handed The incident was captured by broadcasters in the 11th over of Rajasthan’s chase of 202, shortly after Vaibhav Sooryavanshi’s dismissal.
stories.summary: Rajasthan Royals team manager Romi Bhinder is under scrutiny for breaching the Indian Premier League's (IPL) anti-corruption protocol. During the 11th over of Rajasthan's chase of 202 against Royal Challengers Bengaluru in IPL 2026, Bhinder was caught on camera using a mobile phone in the team dugout. Player Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, sitting next to him, was also seen glancing at the device shortly after his dismissal. The incident, captured by broadcasters, has sparked controversy and calls for action, including from former IPL chairman Lalit Modi. Bhinder's actions potentially violate the IPL's strict protocols, and an investigation is likely to follow. The incident has been widely shared on social media, putting the Rajasthan Royals team under pressure to respond to the allegations. The IPL's governing body is expected to take immediate action regarding the breach.

stories.headline: Clippers Make Kawhi Leonard Announcement The LA Clippers have announced an update on Kawhi Leonard before Sunday's game against the Golden State Warriors.
stories.summary: The LA Clippers have listed star player Kawhi Leonard as questionable for their upcoming game against the Golden State Warriors due to a left ankle sprain. Leonard's status is crucial for the Clippers, who are currently sitting in the 9th seed in the Western Conference. The team's announcement has sent shockwaves through the NBA, as his presence or absence could be a make-or-break factor for the Clippers. The update on Leonard's health comes amid the team's ongoing slide in the Western Conference standings. The Clippers are set to face the Warriors on Sunday in their regular-season finale, and Leonard's availability will be closely monitored. His ankle sprain has put his participation in doubt, leaving the team's chances of winning uncertain. The Clippers will likely make a final decision on Leonard's status closer to game time.

stories.headline: La Liga title race: How it stands and remaining fixtures for Barcelona and Real Madrid The Catalans are closing in on more silverware.
stories.summary: Barcelona is closing in on the La Liga title with a 9-point lead over Real Madrid, with 7 games left to play for both teams. The two rivals will meet again in La Liga this season, in El Clásico at Spotify Camp Nou in Matchweek 35, which could be a decisive match. Barcelona recently defeated Espanyol 4-1, with Ferran Torres scoring a double, while Real Madrid drew 1-1 with Girona. The win increased Barcelona's lead, making it likely they will retain the title. Real Madrid's recent poor performances have given Barcelona a significant advantage, with the Catalans now in a strong position to win the league. The two teams have 21 points left to play for, but Barcelona's lead makes them the clear favorites to win the title.

stories.headline: Liverpool vs Fulham LIVE: Premier League result, latest updates and fan reaction Cottagers fail to close in on European places as Reds impress at home
stories.summary: Liverpool secured a 2-0 win over Fulham in their Premier League match at Anfield. The Reds' goals came from 17-year-old Rio Ngumoha and Mohamed Salah in the first half. Ngumoha's goal was a notable moment of magic, featuring two stepovers and two chops before bending the ball into the net. Liverpool's win ended their poor run of form, marking their first Premier League victory since February. Fulham, meanwhile, failed to close in on European places. The Cottagers struggled with set pieces, and their late surge was not enough to change the outcome. Liverpool boss Arne Slot expressed happiness with the win, considering it a massive boost ahead of their next match on Tuesday. The victory gave Liverpool a much-needed three points, with the team dominating the match except for a brief late surge from Fulham.

stories.headline: TS Inter 1st and 2nd Year Results 2026: Official and unofficial websites to check Telangana intermediate marks memo News
stories.summary: The Telangana State Board of Intermediate Education (TSBIE) has released the results for the 1st and 2nd year intermediate exams 2026. Students can check their results on the official website, bse.telangana.gov.in, as well as on other websites such as results.gov.in, manabadi.com, and results.eenadu.net. The results are also available on manabadi.co.in, which provides quick access links to download the marks memo. To access their results, students need to enter their hall ticket number on the official or co-hosting platforms. The results have been released for over 9 lakh students, and candidates can download their marks memo and check the toppers list on the official website, tgbienew.cgg.gov.in. The board has also announced that the results will be released to print media through digital means.

stories.headline: Bengaluru-Mumbai Vande Bharat sleeper train gets railways’ nod; full schedule soon
stories.summary: Indian Railways has approved the introduction of a new Vande Bharat Sleeper train service between Bengaluru and Mumbai. The semi-high-speed train will operate between KSR Bengaluru and Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj Terminus (CSMT) in Mumbai. Railway Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw has granted approval for the service, which aims to improve overnight travel on the busy route while enhancing passenger comfort and overall journey experience. The new train is expected to make long-distance overnight travel faster and more comfortable. The full schedule for the Bengaluru-Mumbai Vande Bharat Sleeper train will be announced soon. The introduction of this service is part of Indian Railways' efforts to upgrade its train services and provide better facilities to passengers. The Vande Bharat Sleeper train will offer a more convenient and comfortable travel option for passengers on the Bengaluru-Mumbai route.

stories.headline: Is right to vote a fundamental right? Supreme Court answers
stories.summary: The Supreme Court of India has ruled that the right to vote and the right to contest elections are statutory rights, not fundamental rights. This means that these rights exist only where provided by law and can be regulated or restricted through legal provisions. A bench of Justices BV Nagarathna and R Mahadevan recently reiterated this stance, emphasizing that the right to vote and the right to contest elections are distinct and statutory entitlements. The court's decision clarifies that these rights are not inherent or absolute, but rather created and governed by law. This ruling has significant implications for the regulation of elections and voting rights in India, as it allows for legal provisions to restrict or modify these rights as needed. The court's decision is based on the principle that statutory rights can be modified or restricted by law.

stories.headline: INTERVIEW | 'DMK that gave way to BJP in Tamil Nadu': Edappadi K Palaniswami
stories.summary: Edappadi K Palaniswami, AIADMK general secretary, stated that the DMK gave way to the BJP in Tamil Nadu. In an interview, he expressed confidence that the NDA will receive overwhelming support in the upcoming Assembly polls. Palaniswami claimed that the AIADMK will form a government on its own, indicating a strong showing for the party. He made these comments in a conversation with T Muruganandham of TNIE. The AIADMK leader's statements suggest a shift in the state's political landscape, with the party aiming to capitalize on the current situation to its advantage. Palaniswami's assertions come as the state prepares for the Assembly elections, with the AIADMK seeking to regain power. The outcome of the polls will determine the future of Tamil Nadu's government.

stories.headline: Stock market outlook: US-Iran talks collapse, oil prices to weigh on sentiment; inflation data, Q4 earnings in focus
stories.summary: The collapse of US-Iran talks is expected to weigh on investor sentiment, driving volatility in the stock market and precious metals. Analysts predict benchmark indices will open with a significant gap down, potentially erasing recent gains. The market will also focus on inflation data, Q4 earnings, and foreign investor activity. Oil prices, which eased ahead of the planned talks, are now likely to rise, further impacting sentiment. The US Producer Price Index (PPI) and speeches from Federal Reserve officials will also be closely watched for direction in bullion prices. The breakdown of negotiations has altered the near-term outlook, and markets are bracing for a return of volatility. Investors will track domestic inflation data and global cues, including crude oil prices, to navigate the uncertain market environment. The holiday-shortened week is expected to be marked by increased volatility.

stories.headline: Inconclusive US-Iran talks, oil prices, inflation data to dictate investors' sentiment: Analysts
stories.summary: US-Iran talks have failed to yield a conclusive outcome, impacting investor sentiment. The collapse of these talks is expected to drive oil prices higher and borrowing costs up when markets open. Analysts predict that crude oil prices and key macroeconomic data, including inflation, will dictate market trends in the coming week. The failed negotiations may lead to increased oil prices, which could further fuel inflation. As a result, investors will be closely watching the developments related to West Asia and the release of inflation data. The reaction of financial markets will be significant, with higher oil prices and borrowing costs likely to influence market outlook. The week ahead is expected to be volatile, with investors awaiting key economic indicators and further updates on the US-Iran situation.

stories.headline: Inconclusive US-Iran talks, oil prices, inflation data to dictate investors’ sentiment: Analysts
stories.summary: Inconclusive US-Iran peace talks and rising crude oil prices are expected to drive market volatility and impact exchange rates. Oil prices dropped 11% after US President Donald Trump postponed military strikes against Iranian power plants, citing constructive talks. The talks, held in Pakistan, ended without a deal and will weigh heavily on investor sentiment. Crude oil prices will also dictate market trends, with soaring prices making investors nervous about potential inflation spikes. US inflation reached 3.3% in March, the fastest annual pace in nearly two years, driven in part by a war-driven jump in gas prices. Analysts say these developments, along with new inflation data, will dictate investor sentiment in a holiday-shortened week, potentially impacting the dollar to rupee exchange rate and overall economic outlook. Market volatility is expected to continue until a resolution is reached.

stories.headline: The talks between Iran and the US in Pakistan, which ended without a deal, would weigh heavily on investors’ sentiment when markets open
stories.summary: The US and Iran failed to reach a peace deal after 21 hours of negotiations in Pakistan. The talks, led by US Vice President JD Vance, ended without an agreement, with Vance stating that Iran "had chosen not to accept our terms." This outcome is expected to weigh heavily on investors' sentiment when markets open, lifting demand for safe-haven assets. US President Trump echoed prior threats, saying he could target Iran's energy infrastructure and that he "could take out Iran in one day." The failed talks are seen as "bad news for Iran much more than for the USA," according to Vance. The lack of a deal will likely impact market sentiment on Monday, with analysts predicting a negative reaction to the news.

stories.headline: Walmart-owned Flipkart, Amazon are squeezing India's quick commerce startups
stories.summary: Walmart-owned Flipkart and Amazon are increasing competition in India's quick commerce sector, putting pressure on startups like Blinkit, Zepto, and Swiggy Instamart. Amazon has rolled out around 450-500 dark stores, with 330-370 currently operational, according to UBS. Flipkart's entry into the market has also intensified the competition. The e-commerce giants' expansion is squeezing India's quick commerce startups, which trade at higher enterprise value-to-sales ratios than most global peers. The Indian quick commerce market is booming, with a potential value of $100 billion. Startups in the sector are facing immense pressure as Flipkart and Amazon ramp up their presence, with Amazon entering the market in late 2024, shortly after Flipkart's debut. The increased competition is expected to impact the valuation and growth of Indian quick commerce startups.

stories.headline: Amazon plans to do an 'impossible' in data centers with its 'Project Houdini', here's what it means
stories.summary: Amazon's Project Houdini aims to accelerate AWS data center builds using modular construction. The initiative involves preassembling key components in factories, reducing on-site labor hours and speeding up deployment. This approach is expected to significantly reduce the time required to build data centers, a crucial step for AI development. Amazon plans to build around 30 data centers over the next several years, equipped with hundreds of thousands of specialized computer chips. The project aims to help the company work through its $244 billion worth of cloud computing backlogs. By relocating much of the construction work to factory-based assembly, Amazon hopes to overcome data center construction bottlenecks and deliver its cloud computing services more efficiently. The modular construction method is expected to increase the speed and efficiency of data center builds.

stories.headline: Local banks expected to limit household loan growth to 1% this year: sources
stories.summary: Local banks are expected to limit household loan growth to 1% this year, according to financial industry sources. The National Agricultural Cooperative Federation (NACF) has issued a directive to its local Nonghyup units, requiring those with year-on-year household loan growth exceeding 1% to halt all household lending to non-members and associate members. This move is part of a broader trend of tightening lending standards, with domestic banks also tightening standards for commercial and industrial loans. The US economy is expected to experience a decline in GDP growth, from 2.8% in 2024 to 1.4% in 2025, which may contribute to the slowdown in loan growth. Household debt remains a significant issue, with 26.4 million people carrying $276 billion in personal loan debt across America.

stories.headline: Q4 results FY26, US-Iran war to crude oil prices: Top five triggers that may dictate the Indian stock market this week
stories.summary: The Indian stock market is expected to be influenced by several key factors this week. The Q4 results for FY26 will be closely watched, along with developments in the US-Iran conflict and its impact on crude oil prices. Geopolitical tensions in the Middle East have already led to a sixth straight week of losses for the Indian stock market. The Sensex and Nifty 50 indices had previously climbed over 1% on Friday, April 10, but then plunged, with the Sensex closing at 78,918.90, down 1,097 points or 1.4%. The US-Iran conflict and crude oil prices will remain major sentiment drivers, affecting market sentiment and potentially leading to further volatility. Other triggers, including global events, will also be monitored for their impact on the Indian stock market this week.

stories.headline: Anthropic steals AI spotlight from OpenAI at HumanX
stories.summary: At the HumanX conference in San Francisco, Anthropic AI stole the spotlight from OpenAI, with its Claude Code tool dominating discussions among the 6,500 attendees. The conference, which gathered executives, founders, and investors to discuss artificial intelligence, saw a shift in interest towards Anthropic. Many acknowledged that while OpenAI, Cursor, and Google offer alternatives, Claude Code has captured attention. This signifies a potential shift in the AI landscape, with Anthropic emerging as a new favorite, surpassing OpenAI. Venture capitalists and industry experts expressed a consensus that Anthropic has gained an edge, although no one is counting out OpenAI due to the rapid pace of AI advancements. The conference highlighted Anthropic's surge in popularity, with many placing bets on the company over OpenAI.

stories.headline: Google Unveils PaLM 2, a More Powerful AI Model
stories.summary: Google has introduced PaLM 2, an updated version of its large language model, with enhanced multilingual, coding, and reasoning capabilities. PaLM 2 has been extensively trained on multilingual text, covering over 100 languages, enabling it to understand, generate, and translate nuanced text, including idioms and poems, across various languages. The model is highly versatile and can adapt to a range of tasks, making it a general-purpose AI. Its capabilities include generating and debugging code, and it can be distilled into smaller models that can run on mobile devices, even offline. Google has already integrated PaLM 2 into its Bard AI, which was initially launched in the US and UK, and has enabled recent developments such as code generation and debugging. PaLM 2 boasts improved performance and capabilities, making it a state-of-the-art language model.

stories.headline: Meta Releases Llama, an AI Designed to Process Human Language
stories.summary: Meta has released Llama, a large language model designed to process human language, at its inaugural AI developer conference, LlamaCon. The conference showcased Meta's latest advancements in open-source AI, including the unveiling of the Llama 4 models. The Llama 4 models, Scout 17B and Maverick 17B, are now available in Amazon Bedrock and Amazon SageMaker JumpStart, and can process both images and text together. The models can intelligently choose which parts to use for each question, making them more efficient. Meta has also launched a standalone AI app built on the Llama 4 model, introducing a new interface and features designed to elevate user interaction and personalization. The app combines text and voice commands, allowing for more powerful applications. The release marks a significant milestone in Meta's AI journey and challenges OpenAI's dominance in the AI chatbot space.

stories.headline: Microsoft Invests $1 Billion in OpenAI to Develop AI Technology
stories.summary: Microsoft is investing $1 billion in OpenAI to support the development of artificial general intelligence (AGI) with widespread economic benefits. The investment will be used to develop a hardware and software platform within Microsoft Azure, scaling to AGI. OpenAI will partner with Microsoft to jointly develop new AI technologies for the Azure cloud platform and enter into an exclusivity agreement to extend large-scale AI capabilities. In exchange, Microsoft will be OpenAI's preferred partner for commercializing new AI technologies. The investment is a cash infusion, with OpenAI's chairman and CTO confirming it will be spent within 5 years. The partnership aims to deliver on the promise of AGI, with Microsoft Azure serving as the platform for OpenAI's AI technologies. The investment and partnership are expected to drive innovation and advancement in AI capabilities.
